title: Pruning function in T5Attention does not affect relative position bucket state
issue_summary: Pruning T5 attention heads removed the projection heads but did not consistently update the relative-position bias path, so pruned inference remained misaligned.

root_cause.summary: Head pruning did not propagate correctly to the relative position bias bookkeeping and masking path.
root_cause.basis: issue report and linked fix PR

symptom: After pruning, the remaining heads use the wrong relative bias slices and produce misaligned scores.

local_reproduction.mapping: The local case prunes selected heads, keeps the weight projections consistent, and contrasts a stale bias slice ordering against a correctly pruned bias tensor.

issue_contract.trigger: Prune a subset of attention heads in a model with relative position bias.
issue_contract.mechanism: The buggy path keeps the pruned projection heads but reuses the wrong relative-bias head slices.
issue_contract.observable_symptom: Pruned attention scores become inconsistent because the bias tensor is misaligned.

reproduction_scope.mechanism_level_claim: This case reproduces head pruning that fails to update the relative-position bias path consistently.
reproduction_scope.issue_level_observable_claim: This case reproduces the reported symptom that pruned attention behaves inconsistently because relative bias stays misaligned.
